messages.content: You output JSON for task plans.

You are a senior project planner producing structured task plans.

Follow this JSON schema:
TaskPlan { originalPrompt: string; subtasks: Subtask[]; totalSubtasks: number; decompositionStrategy?: string }
Subtask { id: string (kebab-case), title: string, description: string (>=10 chars), status: one of pending|in_progress|completed|failed, dependencies: string[], estimatedComplexity: low|medium|high, successCriteria: string }
Constraints: 2-10 subtasks, actionable descriptions, include logical dependencies, avoid circular references, IDs unique.
Original prompt: Framework: FastAPI, Styling: Tailwind CSS

Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text "hello world" colors, through a frontend ui
Clarifications:
- framework: FastAPI
- styling: Tailwind CSS
Previous validation issues:
None
Respond ONLY with JSON matching the schema.

response:
{
  "originalPrompt": "Framework: FastAPI, Styling: Tailwind CSS. Original request: create hello world app, where i can modify the text \"hello world\" colors, through a frontend ui",
  "subtasks": [
    {
      "id": "project-setup",
      "title": "Initialize project and dependencies",
      "description": "Create a new project directory, initialize a Python virtual environment, and add FastAPI, Uvicorn, and any build tools needed for Tailwind (e.g., Node.js, npm) to the project.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "Project folder exists with virtual environment, requirements (or pyproject) listing FastAPI and Uvicorn, and package.json with tailwind-related devDependencies (or notes for installing Node)."
    },
    {
      "id": "backend-app",
      "title": "Implement basic FastAPI app and static file serving",
      "description": "Create a FastAPI app with a root route that serves an index HTML (or template) and configure static files mounting so the frontend assets (CSS, JS) can be served.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "project-setup"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "FastAPI application runs with Uvicorn and returns the index HTML at / while static files under /static or /assets are served successfully."
    },
    {
      "id": "tailwind-setup",
      "title": "Configure Tailwind CSS build pipeline",
      "description": "Install and configure Tailwind (tailwind.config.js, postcss config if needed), set up an input CSS file with Tailwind directives, and add a build script that outputs bundled CSS into the static directory.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "project-setup"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "A built CSS file containing Tailwind utilities is generated in the project's static folder and can be linked from the index HTML to apply Tailwind styles."
    },
    {
      "id": "templates-and-static",
      "title": "Create index HTML and include Tailwind",
      "description": "Build the index HTML or template containing the \"hello world\" text element and include the compiled Tailwind CSS. Add placeholders for the color controls and client-side JS file reference.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "backend-app",
        "tailwind-setup"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "Index page loads in the browser showing the hello world text styled by Tailwind, and the page references the client JS and a UI area for color controls."
    },
    {
      "id": "api-color-endpoint",
      "title": "Add API endpoint to get/update selected color",
      "description": "Implement a simple REST endpoint (e.g., /api/color) to GET the current color and POST/PUT updates. Use in-memory storage (or lightweight file) so color state persists during server runtime.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "backend-app"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "API responds to GET with a JSON { color: \"#...\" } and accepts POST/PUT with a new color value, returning success and updated color."
    },
    {
      "id": "frontend-interactivity",
      "title": "Build UI controls and wire to API with JavaScript",
      "description": "Implement client-side JS to render color picker controls, send changes to the /api/color endpoint, and update the hello world element's Tailwind-compatible classes or inline styles in real time.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "templates-and-static",
        "api-color-endpoint"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "medium",
      "successCriteria": "User can pick a color in the UI which sends the change to the API and immediately updates the hello world text color in the page; subsequent page reload reflects the saved color from GET."
    },
    {
      "id": "testing-and-run",
      "title": "End-to-end test and run instructions",
      "description": "Perform manual end-to-end checks: build Tailwind, run the FastAPI server, open the index page, change colors, and verify persistence within the running server. Document run and build commands.",
      "status": "pending",
      "dependencies": [
        "project-setup",
        "backend-app",
        "tailwind-setup",
        "templates-and-static",
        "api-color-endpoint",
        "frontend-interactivity"
      ],
      "estimatedComplexity": "low",
      "successCriteria": "A developer can follow documented commands to build CSS, start the server, load the app, change the hello world color via UI, and see persistent color during the server session."
    }
  ],
  "totalSubtasks": 7,
  "decompositionStrategy": "Top-down feature decomposition: start with project initialization, then implement backend scaffolding and Tailwind setup in parallel, add templates and API endpoints, then implement frontend interactivity that ties UI to the API, and finish with end-to-end testing and documentation. Dependencies reflect build order and integration points."
}
